channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Judge Rules Jacob Zuma Arms Deal Trial Must Proceed Without Further Delay
item.description: In a significant ruling, Judge Nkosinathi Chili of the KwaZulu-Natal Division of the High Court in Pietermaritzburg has ordered that the long-running arms deal corruption case against former President...

item.title: Trump Concludes Beijing Visit with Limited Trade Success and Discussions on Taiwan and Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump ended his visit to China on May 15, 2026, without major breakthroughs in trade or assistance regarding the Iran conflict, despite spending two days praising Chinese President...

item.title: Canada Eligible to Participate in Eurovision Song Contest Pending Formal Invitation
item.description: Canada could potentially participate in a future Eurovision Song Contest, but no official entry has yet been proposed, according to contest director Martin Green. For Canada to compete, its national b...

item.title: Fuel Prices Go Up, Kenyans Brace for Higher Transport and Living Costs
item.description: Kenyans are feeling the pinch after a significant increase in fuel prices that took effect from midnight. Super Petrol has gone up by Sh16.65 per litre, bringing the new price to Sh214.25, while Diese...

item.title: CAAP Holds Search and Rescue Exercise in Manila Bay to Boost Emergency Preparedness
item.description: The Civil Aviation Authority of the Philippines (CAAP) successfully carried out a search and rescue exercise, known as SAREX, at the dolomite beach along Roxas Boulevard in Manila on May 15, 2026. Thi...

item.title: Harvey Norman current catalogue highlights: Top tech, audio and home deals
item.description: Harvey Norman is currently running a range of attractive discounts across electronics, audio equipment, televisions, laptops and home furniture as part of its latest catalogue sales. Standout offers i...

item.title: Lagos State Food Hubs Achieve ₦3.8 Billion in Transactions as Government Expands Agro Markets
item.description: The Lagos State Government don reveal say their food hub initiative don record over ₦3.8 billion in transactions. According to the Agric Commissioner, Abisola Olusanya, the Idi-Oro Fresh Food Hub for ...

item.title: Maraga, Sifuna and United Opposition agree on one candidate to challenge President Ruto in 2027
item.description: A group of opposition leaders, led by former Chief Justice David Maraga and Nairobi senator Edwin Sifuna, have come together under the United Opposition banner to push for a single presidential candid...

item.title: Early Photographs of Jackie Collins Capturing Her 1950s and 1960s Style
item.description: Before achieving global fame as the 'Queen of Hollywood Glamour,' Jackie Collins was already making a mark in the 1950s and 1960s as a captivating young starlet. These portraits highlight a transforma...

item.title: PCOS renamed PMOS to better reflect its hormonal and metabolic nature
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), a condition affecting around 10–13% of women globally, has been officially renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) following an extensive internationa...

item.title: Ukrainian Puppetry Offers Cultural Resilience and Comfort Amid Conflict
item.description: As the war in Ukraine continues to disrupt everyday life, artists have turned to puppetry, one of the country's oldest storytelling traditions, to provide solace and preserve cultural identity. Puppet...

item.title: BPI Restores Card and ATM Services After Resolving System Outage
item.description: The Bank of the Philippine Islands (BPI) has confirmed that its card and automated teller machine (ATM) services have been fully restored following a recent system outage. According to the announcemen...

item.title: Karachi Police Investigate International Links in Anmol’s Drug Operations
item.description: Karachi police, led by Additional Inspector General Azad Khan, have exposed foreign involvement in the drug network operated by Anmol, also known as Pinky. During a press conference, AIG Khan revealed...

item.title: Senior critically injured after driver rams vehicles and sidewalk in Vancouver's West End
item.description: Vancouver Police responded to an early morning incident on Friday in the West End neighbourhood where a driver allegedly acted erratically, ramming multiple vehicles and driving along a sidewalk. The ...

item.title: Steven Tyler Photographed With His 1956 Mercedes-Benz 220S in Boston, 1975
item.description: In October 1975, photographer Ron Pownall captured images of Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler with his 1956 Mercedes-Benz 220S convertible at Jamaica Pond in Boston, Massachusetts. The photos were take...

item.title: PCCB Recovers 167 Million Shillings from Probed Projects Worth 8.23 Billion in Singida Region
item.description: The Prevention and Combating of Corruption Bureau (PCCB) in Singida Region has taken strong action against corruption in development projects. Officers probed several projects valued at 8.23 billion T...

item.title: NordVPN and other tech companies may leave Canada over privacy concerns in proposed lawful access bill
item.description: The Canadian federal government has introduced a proposed 'lawful access' bill that has sparked significant concern among privacy-focused technology companies. NordVPN, a widely used virtual private n...

item.title: Understanding Decision Fatigue and Its Effects on Healthy Eating Choices
item.description: This article explains the concept of decision fatigue and how it affects our daily food choices. Decision fatigue happens when we make too many decisions throughout the day, using up our mental energy...

item.title: Behind the Scenes with James Caan on the Set of The Godfather (1972)
item.description: James Caan’s experience on the set of The Godfather (1972) was a mix of high-energy improvisation, playful pranks, and intense physical scenes. Originally considered for the role of Michael Corleone, ...

item.title: Pakistanis and Iranians Repatriated from US-Seized Vessels with Singapore and Thailand’s Help
item.description: On Friday, Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar confirmed that 11 Pakistanis and 20 Iranians, who were aboard vessels recently seized by the United States in international waters, have...
